name: Cobra
what_it_does: The standard Go CLI framework used by kubectl, Docker, GitHub CLI, Hugo, and hundreds of production CLIs. Provides nested subcommand support, flags (persistent and local), automatic help generation, shell completion (bash, zsh, fish, PowerShell), and a cobra-cli generator tool. The de facto standard for Go CLI applications.
use_cases: Build multi-subcommand agent CLI tools (agent run, agent config, agent logs) with nested command hierarchies
Add shell completion to agent CLI interfaces for improved operator experience
Implement persistent flags that apply to all subcommands and local flags scoped to specific commands
Create Go CLI interfaces for agent services following the kubectl/Docker CLI pattern
Generate project scaffolding with cobra-cli generator for new agent CLI projects
not_for: Simple single-command CLIs with one or two flags — flag package or pflag alone may be sufficient
Interactive terminal UIs — cobra handles argument parsing only; use bubbletea or promptui for TUI/interactive features
WASM environments — cobra requires OS I/O
best_when: You're building a Go CLI tool with multiple subcommands following the kubectl/Docker pattern — Cobra is the universal standard in the Go CLI ecosystem.
avoid_when: You have a very simple CLI with no subcommands — the standard library flag package or pflag alone is lighter weight.

pricing.notes: Apache 2.0 license. Created by Steve Francia (spf13). Powers kubectl, Hugo, GitHub CLI.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits — startup-time argument parsing only.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Unknown command and flag errors produce clear user-facing messages with suggestions. Required flag missing errors include the flag name. Help text auto-generated from command descriptions.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: CLI argument parsing — deterministic, no side effects from the framework itself.
known_agent_gotchas: cobra uses pflag for flags (not the standard library flag package) — flag syntax uses --flag=value and -f shorthand; standard library single-dash flags (-flag) are not supported
Persistent flags defined on the root command are available to ALL subcommands — local flags are only available on the specific command they're attached to
cobra.Command.RunE returns an error that cobra catches and prints — don't os.Exit() directly inside RunE; return the error and cobra handles exit codes
Shell completion generation changed in cobra 1.x — use RegisterFlagCompletionFunc() and ValidArgsFunction for custom completion; older completion APIs are deprecated
Command use string (e.g., "serve") is used in help text and error messages — keep it short and lowercase; the Use string becomes part of the help syntax shown to users
viper integration for config file binding requires explicit BindPFlag() calls after flags are defined — flag values don't automatically merge with viper config without explicit binding

security_score_components.security_notes: CLI framework. Sensitive values passed as flags appear in process lists — prefer environment variables for secrets. No network calls in framework itself.
